message:
[LOW] fcp-crypto: reject reconstruct_secret inputs larger than 255 shares

ShamirShare::index is u8, and index 0 is reserved for the secret itself,
so at most 255 distinct shares can ever be meaningful input to
reconstruct_secret. The existing duplicate-index check caught the issue
*after* doing O(N) scans over the input slice — a caller handing in a
million (mostly-duplicate) shares paid full O(N) cost before discovering
the violation.

Gate with an early pigeonhole check: `shares.len() > 255 =>
TooManyShares`. Zero cost on legitimate inputs, constant-time rejection
on pathological ones.

Test: reconstruct_error_too_many_shares passes a 256-element slice with
cycled valid indices and asserts TooManyShares is returned before any
per-share work runs.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-crypto --lib reconstruct → all reconstruct_*
tests green including the new case.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
